description: Execute a plan until it is complete

steps.label: Planner Select And Store Next Plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk now, using explicit shell reads such as `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`, and read the whole selected plan again before judging the tasking output, because another agent has just edited it. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. The plan has now been tasked up. Confirm the tasking output against the freshly re-read plan on disk, including any new tasks, subtasks, testing sections, manual-testing guidance, documentation locations, and the simple story that were just added or updated. Please check that the tasks created fully cover that selected plan and that nothing has been missed. Ensure the tasking followed the current task-up rules, including portable non-absolute paths outside concrete local `Additional Repositories` entries, test-only auth or login enablement, final lint then prettier subtasks, final lint then prettier testing steps, manual-testing preference for the unmodified human Docker stack when feasible, ignored artifact paths for automated screenshots, consistent `codeInfoTmp/` and `codeInfoStatus/` casing, and a `.gitignore` entry for `codeInfoTmp/` whenever non-final manual-proof artifacts are planned. If you feel something is missing, please update the plan, tasks, and simple story accordingly, and if you make changes in this audit step then create a follow-up commit for those edits before finishing. Remember to ensure that each task has clear implementation instructions and testing steps so a developer can fully understand what to do. Please confirm once you are happy that the plan has been fully tasked up and nothing is missing. Do not ask me any questions, instead just update the plan and tasks following the KISS principle until you are happy everything is fully covered.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after tasking

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk even if you have read it before, using explicit shell reads such as `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. Re-check the current repository branch directly from git, for example with `git branch --show-current`, and re-check the most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git log --oneline -1`. If either of those scope checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. Re-check each additional repository branch directly from git, for example with `git -C <repo_root> branch --show-current`, and re-check its most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git -C <repo_root> log --oneline -1`. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. You MUST double-check to see if tasks have been added to the plan by another agent or if anything has changed. You must ensure you fully understand the plan and its remaining tasks before proceeding. You MUST read the whole plan again and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of the remaining work.

steps.label: Task Execution And Blocker Repair Loop
Exit if no implementation task is available
steps.question: Answer from the selector result you just produced in the immediately preceding step. Answer yes only if you just wrote a state that does NOT resolve a current task for implementation, for example because plan repair is needed or the story is already complete. Answer no only if you just resolved one current task for this loop pass. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for blocker before automated proof
steps.question: Use the same bound task from the immediately preceding deep repair step. If that step did not clearly resolve one bound task, answer yes. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <that-same-bound-task-number>`. Answer from that JSON output only. Answer yes iff `selected_task` is missing or `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. Answer no only if `selected_task` exists and `selected_task.live_blockers` is empty. The parser already ignores inline `**BLOCKER**` references, `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Run automated proof and fix issues
Check manual testing eligibility
steps.question: Use the same bound task from the immediately preceding automated-proof audit step. If that step did not clearly resolve one bound task, answer yes. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <that-same-bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff any of the following are true: `selected_task` is missing; `selected_task.has_unchecked_subtasks` is true; `selected_task.has_unchecked_testing` is true; or `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. Answer no only when the selected task exists, has no unchecked subtasks, has no unchecked testing, and has no live blockers, because only then should the task continue to the manual-testing phase whether its status is `__in_progress__` or `__done__`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for blocker before manual testing
steps.question: Use the same bound task from the immediately preceding automated-proof audit step. If that step did not clearly resolve one bound task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <that-same-bound-task-number>`. Answer from that JSON output only. Answer yes iff `selected_task` exists and `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. The parser already ignores inline `**BLOCKER**` references, `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for blocker after manual testing
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer from that JSON output only. Answer yes iff `selected_task` exists and `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. The parser already ignores inline `**BLOCKER**` references, `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check if manual testing added follow-up work
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes only if that bound task now has unchecked subtasks or unchecked testing steps. Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after normalization
Research blocker solution and prove it
Refresh current plan handoff after planner edits

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`. Verify that every repository in scope is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename or report drift if it does not. Then run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --include-tasks` and use that JSON output as the source of truth for task state. Before answering completion, list each task's Task Status from the parser output, explicitly state whether the final task is __done__ or not using `final_task_status`, cite the relative `plan_path`, and state whether repository branch scope still matches the selected story.

steps.label: Check for completion
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk, for example with `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`, before answering so you are not relying on memory. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then check the last 3 git commits in the current repository and in every additional repository, and run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the parser reports `all_tasks_done: true` and `story_complete: true` and every repository in scope is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Minor-Fix Path Unless Minor Findings Remain
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`, and answer only from that file's current contents. Answer yes if `needs_minor_fix_path` is not true. Answer no only when `needs_minor_fix_path` is true, meaning unresolved minor-batchable findings still remain and should continue to be attempted inline before task-up. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Minor-Fix Path After Sync Unless Another Minor Finding Remains
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk after the sync step, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`, and read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/minor-review-fix-result.json` from disk too, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/minor-review-fix-result.json`. Answer only from those freshly re-read files. Answer yes iff `needs_minor_fix_path` is not true, meaning no unresolved minor-batchable findings remain or the remaining minor findings were bulk-escalated out of the minor bucket. Answer no only if `needs_minor_fix_path` is true, meaning another unresolved minor-batchable finding still needs an inline attempt in this same pass. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Task-Up Path Unless Unresolved Findings Need Tasks
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`, and answer only from that file's current contents. Answer yes iff `needs_task_up_path` is not true. Treat `resolved_minor_findings` as non-actionable for task-up. Answer no only when `needs_task_up_path` is true, meaning unresolved task-required findings or incomplete-review blockers must be encoded into the plan. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Ensure Unresolved Review Findings Became Tasks
Refresh Current Plan Handoff After Review Task-Up
Ensure Review-Created Testing Steps Are Correct
Create Or Update Simple Story After Review Task-Up
Exit Task-Up Path After Encoding Review Findings
steps.question: Answer yes after the task-up path has either encoded unresolved review findings into the plan or recorded why that path cannot continue. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"}.

steps.label: Exit Review Loop After Serious Review Work Was Tasked Up
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`. Then read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, determine `plan_path`, and re-open that exact plan from disk using `sed`, `cat`, or `rg` before answering. Answer only from those freshly re-read files. Answer yes iff the current review loop added or updated numbered review-fix tasks for unresolved task-required findings or incomplete-review blockers, meaning the flow should leave the review loop and return to the main implementation loop. Answer no otherwise, including when no numbered task was added or when only a post-loop final minor-fix revalidation task may still need to be considered. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Review Loop When No Further Minor Rerun Is Needed
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`, and answer only from that file's current contents. Answer no iff `needs_review_rerun_before_close` is true and no serious review task-up work was added, because the review must run again against the new HEAD before the loop can finish. Answer yes iff `needs_review_rerun_before_close` is false, meaning the review loop may now finish cleanly and let downstream flow steps decide whether a post-loop final minor-fix revalidation task is needed. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Generate Or Update Final Revalidation Task For Minor Fixes
Checkpoint Push Before Story Completion Check
Check for completion
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk, for example with `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`, before answering so you are not relying on memory. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then check the last 3 git commits in the current repository and in every additional repository, and run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the parser reports `all_tasks_done: true` and `story_complete: true`. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.
